question.text: As per SEBI (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993, the registration fee payable at the time of grant of certificate of registration as a Debenture Trustee is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Every debenture trustee shall pay fees of twenty lakh rupees at the time of grant of certificate of registration. The recurring fee is Rs. 9 lakh every three years from the sixth year. **The Trap:** Rs. 50 lakh is the registration fee for Custodians (not Debenture Trustees), and Rs. 15 lakh is the recurring fee for Credit Rating Agencies. **Cross-Exam Context:** Registration fees across intermediaries are commonly compared in NISM Series III-A exam questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The non-refundable application fee is Rs. 50,000, payable along with the application form -- this is separate from the registration fee.
